sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with a 5-day history of right knee pain following a skiing accident, where they twisted their knee while landing a jump and felt a stretching sensation.
The pain has been accompanied by swelling, and the patient has taken Aleve twice a day and applied a cold pack for relief.
The patient denies numbness or tingling in their foot.
The patient has a history of high blood pressure, for which they are currently taking lisinopril 20mg daily.
Past medical history: The patient has had a tonsillectomy in the past.
Review of systems: The patient does not report any additional symptoms.
Current medications: Lisinopril 20mg daily.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 120/77.
Physical exam: The patient has ecchymosis, edema, and an effusion present on the right knee.
There is pain to palpation of the right medial knee.
The patient has decreased range of motion, a negative varus and valgus test, a negative Lachman sign, and palpable dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulses.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a medial collateral ligament strain of the right knee.
Problem list: Medial collateral ligament strain of the right knee; Hypertension; Past medical history of tonsillectomy.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg once daily to help reduce swelling and alleviate pain.
I ordered an x-ray of the right knee to rule out any fractures.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee and prevent future injuries.
I will continue the patient on lisinopril 20mg daily for their hypertension.
I ordered a lipid panel to check for any potential issues related to the patient's hypertension medication.
I administered a tetanus shot for health maintenance.
Follow-up: I will follow up with the patient to discuss the results of the x-ray and physical therapy, and to review any further management plans.
